# daytona_revoke_sandbox_ssh_access (Action)

Revokes Daytona sandbox SSH access.

## Example Usage

```terraform
action "daytona_revoke_sandbox_ssh_access" "example" {
  config {
    sandbox_id_or_name = "sandbox-id-or-name"
    token              = "ssh-access-token"
  }
}
```

<!-- action schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `sandbox_id_or_name` (String) Daytona sandbox ID or name.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `organization_id` (String) Daytona organization ID to send as `X-Daytona-Organization-ID` for this action. Defaults to the provider-level organization ID when configured.
- `token` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Optional SSH access token to revoke. When omitted, Daytona revokes all SSH access for the sandbox. This value is write-only and is not returned by the provider.
